semantic_search.top_results_summary: Semantic search confirmed the dispatcher surface (cmd/atcr/main.go command tree, skill/SKILL.md), the --output-dir contract (docs/code-review-backend.md, internal/fanout/reviewdir.go, cmd/atcr/review_test.go, cmd/atcr/reconcile_test.go), and the existing self-test/onboarding commands (cmd/atcr/doctor.go, cmd/atcr/quickstart.go, internal/quickstart/workflow.go). No install.sh exists anywhere; examples/ci-gate.sh remains the only shell style precedent.

build_from.reason: This is the exact standalone skill Proposed Solution #2 requires converting into a 'single dispatcher skill' using a /atcr <command> UX pattern. It is currently authored as a full linear orchestration script (Prerequisites -> Input Format -> Orchestration Steps -> Host Review Instructions -> Ambiguity Adjudication -> Findings Format Reference), not a command router.
build_from.suggested_approach: Restructure skill/SKILL.md into a lightweight dispatcher: a top-level /atcr <command> <flags> entry point that maps user intent to the underlying atcr CLI commands (review, reconcile, report, status, doctor, quickstart, version, range, etc.), moving the detailed per-flow instructions (host review, ambiguity adjudication, findings format) into secondary markdown files loaded on demand to keep the primary SKILL.md under the ~500-line budget noted in the 2026-07-05 addendum. Use the exact command names registered in cmd/atcr/main.go:185-208 so the router stays in sync with the binary.

existing_patterns.name: Documented CLI contract pages
existing_patterns.description: Stable behavioral contracts (e.g. --output-dir tree shape) are documented as standalone docs/*.md pages separate from README.md, with an explicit 'Output tree' code block and a 'Behavioral notes for callers' section.
existing_patterns.follow_for: Any new documentation this epic adds (install.sh usage, dispatcher command reference) should follow the same doc-per-concern structure and cross-link from README.md's Documentation section.

existing_patterns.name: Cobra subcommand + _test.go pairing
existing_patterns.description: Each cmd/atcr/*.go subcommand has a co-located *_test.go exercising CLI behavior, including --output-dir cases.
existing_patterns.follow_for: The new repo-local backward-compat contract test (AC3) should follow this pairing convention rather than inventing a new test harness.

existing_patterns.name: Cobra root command registration
existing_patterns.description: Subcommands are registered centrally in newRootCmd (cmd/atcr/main.go:185-208) with exact Use strings and cobra.Command structs. This is the canonical command surface the dispatcher must mirror.
existing_patterns.follow_for: The /atcr <command> dispatcher in skill/SKILL.md must route to the exact command names registered here (review, reconcile, report, status, doctor, quickstart, version, range, etc.) and must not invent aliases that drift from the CLI.

existing_patterns.name: Versioned findings stream contract
existing_patterns.description: The per-source and reconciled findings streams are documented in docs/findings-format.md with the exact # atcr-findings/v1 header, 8-column per-source shape, 9-column reconciled shape, and escaping rules.
existing_patterns.follow_for: Host-review and ambiguity-adjudication secondary skill files must reference this contract verbatim; the dispatcher should not re-specify column semantics.

integration_points.description: The dispatcher rewrite target — the `name`/`description` frontmatter and all body sections are the surface being restructured into a /atcr <command> router.
The canonical atcr CLI command tree. The skill dispatcher's command namespace must stay aligned with these registrations.
The documented output tree is the exact contract the new repo-local backward-compat test must assert against by actually running `atcr review --output-dir` + `atcr reconcile`.
The versioned findings stream contract that host-review and adjudication secondary skill files must reference.
End-user install/usage guide for the standalone skill; must remain accurate after the dispatcher rewrite.

The private skill migration (Proposed Solution #3) cannot be written from this workspace. The dispatcher rewrite in this repo should include a documented manual migration checklist, but the actual claude-prompts skill files are out of scope for automated file writes.

reusable_components.usage_example: Use as a style/structure reference (not a code dependency) when authoring install.sh, since it is the only shell script currently shipped in the repo.

reusable_components.name: atcr doctor / atcr quickstart
reusable_components.usage_example: Cite directly in documentation as already satisfying the self-test / quick-start requirement — do not wrap or extend these commands.

reusable_components.name: docs/findings-format.md
reusable_components.usage_example: Cite from secondary skill markdown files instead of redefining the findings stream contract.

architecture_notes: atcr is a single Go binary with three faces (CLI, MCP server via `atcr serve`, and an Agent Skill); skill/SKILL.md is the only existing skill artifact today, authored as a full linear orchestration script rather than a command dispatcher.

The --output-dir contract is already implemented and unit-tested (cmd/atcr/review_test.go, reconcile_test.go, internal/fanout/reviewdir_test.go); this epic's AC3 needs an end-to-end assertion that the documented tree in docs/code-review-backend.md stays stable, not new engine code.
No install.sh or release automation exists anywhere in the repo (confirmed via find); `go install` is the only documented install path today (README.md Quickstart, CHANGELOG 1.x entries).

The private claude-prompts skills (execute-code-review, reconcile-code-review) live in an external repository this workspace cannot write to — Proposed Solution #3's dispatcher migration for those skills is a manual operator action outside this plan's file-write scope.

To keep skill/SKILL.md under the ~500-line budget, the dispatcher should load detailed host-review, ambiguity-adjudication, and findings-format instructions from secondary markdown files (e.g., skill/host-review.md, skill/ambiguity-adjudication.md, skill/findings-format.md) rather than inlining them.
architecture_recommendations: Keep the primary skill/SKILL.md under ~500 lines by moving detailed host-review, adjudication, and findings-format instructions into secondary markdown files loaded on demand.
Mirror the exact command names registered in cmd/atcr/main.go (review, reconcile, report, status, doctor, quickstart, version, range, etc.) in the /atcr dispatcher; avoid inventing aliases.
Make install.sh wrap `go install github.com/samestrin/atcr/cmd/atcr@latest` only, with no pinning logic, release checks, or packaging automation — Epic 21.0 owns that.

Write the AC3 backward-compat contract test as a standard *_test.go in cmd/atcr/ following the review_test.go/reconcile_test.go pattern; use build tag `//go:build integration` if it shells out to a real `go run ./cmd/atcr` binary.
Do not extend or wrap cmd/atcr/doctor.go or cmd/atcr/quickstart.go to satisfy the self-test/quick-start requirement; only update documentation citations.
